title: service_plan Model
description: AI-scaffolded schema for JSON-driven service_plan definitions — verify field constraints before use.

service_plans.description: Map keyed by a stable logical id. The key becomes the for_each key in Terraform.

resource_group_name.description: Must match the "name" of an entry in resource-group.json.

os_type.description: The operating system type for the service plan, either 'Windows' or 'Linux'.

sku_name.description: The pricing tier and size for the service plan, such as 'B1' (Basic), 'S1' (Standard), 'P1V2' (Premium V2), or 'Y1' (Dynamic for Functions).

app_service_environment_id.description: Optional ID of an App Service Environment (isolated, dedicated environment) where this service plan should be created.

maximum_elastic_worker_count.description: The maximum number of workers that can be elastically scaled out for this service plan when using elastic scale-out SKUs.

per_site_scaling_enabled.description: When enabled (true), allows individual apps in this service plan to be scaled independently rather than scaling all apps together.

worker_count.description: The number of worker instances to allocate to the service plan, controlling how many virtual machines run your applications.

zone_balancing_enabled.description: When enabled (true), distributes worker instances across multiple availability zones for improved availability and resilience.
